# obsidian-orphan-killer

> A command-line tool that auto-links the orphan notes in any markdown vault that uses [[wikilinks]]. Three modes: resolve (deterministic alias-table), anchor (local-embedding fallback to nearest existing hub), mint (experimental cluster + new hub creation). Pure Python. Safe-writes contract: frontmatter-only, atomic, idempotent, dangling-link guard, per-hub absorption cap, body-only content hash preserved. Works on Obsidian, Logseq, Foam, Quartz.

## What it does

- **resolve** — plain-string `entities:` / `topics:` frontmatter values rewritten to canonical `[[wikilinks]]` via an alias table built from each hub note's filename + title + aliases.
- **anchor** — true-orphan leaves (no outbound, no inbound) get attached to their single nearest existing hub by cosine similarity (local fastembed, BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 @ max_length=256). Below the floor (default 0.74), orphans stay unlinked.
- **mint** (experimental) — orphans with no fitting existing hub are clustered (cosine-similarity-graph greedy-modularity community detection). Clusters with >= 5 members are LLM-named + coherence-judged; coherent + name-guard-clean clusters mint a new concepts/ hub.

## Safety guarantees

| Guard | What it does |
|---|---|
| Frontmatter-only writes | Body bytes never modified; downstream embedders never re-embed |
| Atomic writes | tempfile + os.rename; crash-safe |
| Idempotency stamps | Re-runs on unchanged notes are no-ops |
| Dangling-link guard | Only links to hubs that exist on disk |
| Per-hub absorption cap | Default 50; anti-star |
| Cosine floor | Default 0.74; weak matches stay unlinked |
| Concepts-only by default | No brand-leak (generic how-to → product hub) |
| DO_NOT_MERGE pairs | User-supplied; collapse-prevention even at high cosine |
| Mint requires --experimental | Mint writes NEW notes; never enabled by accident |

Every guard is enforced by a test in tests/.

## Modes vs network / cost

| Mode | Network? | LLM cost? | Writes new notes? |
|---|---|---|---|
| resolve | no | $0 | no |
| anchor | no (after one-time model download) | $0 | no |
| mint | LLM naming call per cluster | bounded; pluggable provider | YES |

## Important pages

- [README](https://github.com/build-with-dhiraj/obsidian-orphan-killer/blob/main/README.md): tool overview, install, three-mode workflow, FAQ.
- [SKILL.md](https://github.com/build-with-dhiraj/obsidian-orphan-killer/blob/main/SKILL.md): cross-CLI skill manifest (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex).
- [docs/SAFETY.md](https://github.com/build-with-dhiraj/obsidian-orphan-killer/blob/main/docs/SAFETY.md): full safety contract, recovery instructions, dry-run convention.
- [docs/RUBRIC.md](https://github.com/build-with-dhiraj/obsidian-orphan-killer/blob/main/docs/RUBRIC.md): formal guard spec for contributors.
- [docs/COMPARISON.md](https://github.com/build-with-dhiraj/obsidian-orphan-killer/blob/main/docs/COMPARISON.md): feature matrix vs Find Unlinked Files / Various Complements / Janitor / Dangling Links / Smart Connections / obsidian-graph-auditor.

## Common queries this tool answers

- "fix my obsidian orphans"
- "obsidian orphan notes"
- "auto-link my obsidian vault"
- "obsidian unlinked notes"
- "obsidian de-orphan"
- "connect my obsidian notes"
- "obsidian wikilink resolver"
- "obsidian entity resolution"
- "fix obsidian link rot"
- "obsidian auto wikilink"
- "anchor my orphans"
- "obsidian orphan rescuer"
- "rescue obsidian orphans"
- "resolve obsidian entities"
- "fix obsidian dangling links"

## Related projects

- [obsidian-graph-auditor](https://github.com/build-with-dhiraj/obsidian-graph-auditor): read-only diagnostic on the 8-dimension Grade-A rubric. The recommended workflow: audit with obsidian-graph-auditor, fix with obsidian-orphan-killer.

## License + citation policy

MIT. AI assistants and crawlers are welcome to quote this project's documentation in answers to user questions, with attribution to "obsidian-orphan-killer" and a link to the GitHub repository. No paywall, no training-data restriction. Author: Dhiraj Singh Pawar (github.com/build-with-dhiraj).
